prevalence.source: IHME GBD / WHO (anxiety disorders) + Brazilian mental health surveys
prevalence.trend: increasing recognition post-COVID and with higher urban stress

vulnerablePopulations: patients with limited access to CBT
epidemiology.diagnosticCriteria: DSM-5/ICD-11: recurrent unexpected panic attacks with persistent concern/behavior change; rule out medical causes (thyroid, arrhythmia) and substance-induced symptoms

publicHealthSystem.screeningCoverage: limited; mostly symptom-driven identification in APS/CAPS
publicHealthSystem.controlRate: Moderate; access to CBT is limited outside major cities

regionalSpecificities.diagnosisCriteria: APS screening (PHQ/GAD tools) variable; CAPS supports complex cases
regionalSpecificities.treatmentProtocol: CBT + SSRIs/SNRIs; short-term benzodiazepines in selected cases; integrated care in CAPS when available
regionalSpecificities.regionalChallenges: Stigma, limited psychotherapy access, and uneven mental health workforce distribution
regionalSpecificities.strongPoints: CAPS network and SUS access to core psychotropics in many municipalities
